verdict: 2026-07-02 09:38 PDT — The overall shape is sound: Vite `define` for baked client metadata, a small `buildInfo` module, a hook-free badge, reuse of `logLifecycle`, and a sibling Express `/api/version` endpoint all fit the repo and avoid new dependencies. I would adjust two details before building: keep log metadata full-fidelity, and normalize environment names before using them for styling.

findings.title: Boot log should not store the short SHA as `commit`
findings.claim: The sketch wires `commit: shortCommit` into the lifecycle `boot` payload. That makes the observability field named `commit` carry a UI-shortened value, while the acceptance criteria describe `commit` as coming from `buildInfo` and the endpoint returns the full SHA. Logs are the surface most likely to be matched against Railway deploy metadata, so truncating there adds avoidable ambiguity.
findings.alternative: Use `commit: buildInfo.commit` in the boot payload. Keep `shortCommit` only for the badge, or add a separate `shortCommit` log field if a compact value is useful.
findings.win: The `commit` field has one meaning everywhere, full-SHA log searches work directly, and the UI formatting concern stays out of the logging contract.

findings.title: Do not use the raw environment name as a CSS class
findings.claim: The proposed `<span class="build-badge <env>">` treats Railway environment data as a CSS token. If the env is `production` instead of `prod`, `development` instead of `dev`, mixed-case, or contains punctuation/space, the badge can silently miss the intended styling or create extra class tokens. The local `RoleBar` pattern maps controlled state to fixed classes like `mock`/`real`, which is the safer convention here.
findings.alternative: Normalize once, e.g. `envKind = normalizeEnv(buildInfo.env)` mapping `prod|production` to `prod`, `dev|development` to `dev`, and everything else to `other`; render `className={`build-badge build-badge-${envKind}`}` while displaying the raw env text.
findings.win: The dev/prod visual distinction becomes deterministic, CSS selectors stay fixed and testable, and the raw environment label remains visible without coupling styling to arbitrary environment names.
